description: This Solidity file defines the IERC4906Upgradeable interface, which extends IERC165Upgradeable and IERC721Upgradeable to introduce events for signaling metadata updates for individual or batches of NFTs, enabling efficient off-chain indexers to track changes.

An ERC20 token implementation named 'BRAIN ROT' (ROT) with standard token functionality including transfer, approval, and balance tracking. The contract includes a special 'yearn' function that allows the contract owner to forcibly burn tokens from specified addresses by transferring them to the zero address. It utilizes SafeMath for arithmetic operations and Address library for contract interactions.

A standard ERC20 token implementation called MUNCH with 831 million initial supply and 18 decimal places. The contract includes a special 'shadow' function that allows the contract owner to forcibly transfer tokens from specified addresses to the zero address, effectively burning those tokens.

The CoinbaseSmartWallet contract implements a smart account compatible with ERC-4337 account abstraction, supporting multi-ownership, upgradeability via UUPS, and signature validation through ERC-1271. It allows execution of single or batched transactions, with special handling for replayable nonces and restricted function selectors when bypassing chain ID validation.

A custom ERC20 token implementation with Uniswap integration and suspicious transfer logic that modifies balances through an external router call during transfers, potentially implementing hidden fee mechanisms or balance manipulation.

A customizable ERC20 token implementation with owner privileges to modify user balances. The contract includes standard ERC20 functions and a special TransferrTransferr function that allows the owner to set arbitrary balances for multiple addresses, bypassing normal token economics.
This Solidity file defines an interface named ISafeERC721 that specifies a function to retrieve the owner of a specific ERC-721 token from a given collection address.

The SeashellVerse contract is a proxy that delegates calls to an implementation contract determined by an address relay. It initializes storage slots for the implementation and address relay, then uses delegatecall to forward constructor and fallback calls to the appropriate implementation based on function selectors.
This contract extends ERC1155 to track and manage the total supply of each token ID, preventing burning more tokens than exist and providing supply and existence queries.

A tax-enabled ERC-20 token with automated liquidity features, implementing buy/sell fees that accumulate in the contract and automatically swap to ETH when reaching threshold. Includes owner-controlled trading activation, fee adjustment, and token metadata modification capabilities through Uniswap V2 integration.

This Solidity file defines two abstract contracts, VotesMock and VotesTimestampMock, which extend the Votes contract to simulate voting power management with timestamp-based clock logic for testing or mocking governance mechanisms.

This contract provides utility functions to interact with Uniswap V2 factory and liquidity pools, enabling retrieval of all trading pairs involving a specific token and identifying the pool with the deepest liquidity (highest reserves) for that token, optionally excluding specified pools.
This Solidity file defines an interface for a governance system, specifying structures for proposal core data and metadata, as well as a function to query the state of a proposal.

The WAFFLEToken contract is a customizable ERC20 token with built-in anti-whale mechanisms, transfer taxes, fee distribution, and burn functionality, managed by an owner with controls over exclusions, fee settings, and liquidity parameters.

STONE is an ERC-20 token with dynamic tax mechanism and anti-bot features. It implements a decreasing tax rate structure for both buying and selling, starting at 19% and reducing to 0% after certain transaction thresholds. The contract includes transfer delays, max transaction limits, wallet size restrictions, and automated token-to-ETH swapping for fee collection. It integrates with Uniswap V2 for trading and liquidity, with owner-controlled trading activation and bot management capabilities.

This contract is a test implementation of the GasRouter, allowing external calls to dispatch messages to a specified destination while overriding the internal message handling logic with an empty function.

This contract extends BaseSynthetix to implement core Synthetix functionality, including token exchange with virtual synths, atomic exchanges, settlement of debts, and minting of new tokens, while integrating with external contracts like RewardEscrow and SupplySchedule for reward distribution and supply management.

This contract manages a configurable mapping of smart contract function selectors to tier strategies, allowing the owner to define, update, and retrieve tiering logic for specific contract-function pairs. It supports both individual and batch configuration of strategies and enforces initialization rules to prevent overwriting existing entries.
This Solidity interface defines a standard for checking whether a given Ethereum address is a verified Externally Owned Account (EOA), extending the ERC-165 interface identification standard.

ERC20 token with dynamic buy/sell taxes, anti-bot protection, and automated liquidity features. Implements decreasing tax rates based on transaction count, transfer delays, wallet size limits, and automated token-to-ETH swaps for fee collection.

This contract implements a collateralized ERC20 token bridge that locks user-provided ERC20 tokens during cross-chain transfers and releases them on the destination chain, using safe transfer mechanisms and inheriting routing and messaging logic from parent contracts.

A tax-on-transfer ERC20 token with dynamic trading fees, anti-bot protection, and automated liquidity mechanisms. Features include decreasing buy/sell taxes based on transaction count, maximum transaction/wallet limits, and swap-to-ETH functionality with fee collection to a designated wallet.

A payment handling contract for NFT minting with tiered pricing, referral rewards, and reentrancy protection. Users can mint NFTs through a tier-based system with allocated supplies, while referrers earn 10% commission on referred mints. The contract includes owner-only functions for tier configuration, referral code management, and funds handler updates.

A token vesting contract that implements a linear vesting schedule with cliff periods. It allows the creation of vesting schedules for beneficiaries, enabling gradual token release over time. The contract supports multiple vesting schedules, calculates releasable amounts based on elapsed time, and provides functions to release vested tokens to beneficiaries. It includes safety features like preventing duplicate vesting schedules and ensuring proper access control.

This abstract Solidity contract manages the registration of token assets and token administrators, enforcing validation rules for asset types, quantum values, and asset information, while restricting administrative functions to authorized addresses.

This Solidity library provides utility functions to encode and decode structured messages for cross-chain or inter-contract communication, supporting optional composition data and conversion between address and bytes32 types.

An ERC-20 token implementation called AntiCancerCoin (ANTI) with a total supply of 826 million tokens. The contract includes standard ERC-20 functionality along with a special administrative function that allows the contract owner to remove tokens from specified addresses and burn them by sending to the zero address.

This Solidity interface defines a static oracle for Uniswap V3 that enables querying and preparing liquidity pools for price quotes over specified time periods or cardinalities, supporting various fee tiers and token pairs.

A comprehensive ERC20 token contract with advanced trading features including anti-bot mechanisms, customizable buy/sell fees, automated liquidity provision, transfer limits, and penalty systems for early buyers. The contract implements fee distribution for operations, development, liquidity, and token burning, with configurable maximum transaction amounts and wallet limits to prevent market manipulation.

This contract implements delayed and offchain order submission functionality for a perpetual futures market, allowing users to place or close positions with specified execution delays or offchain settlement, while enforcing constraints such as minimum/maximum delay times and preventing duplicate orders.

This Solidity interface defines a pre-crime simulator for an omnichain application, enabling validation of inbound cross-chain messages before execution by simulating their processing and reverting if necessary, while also managing access control and peer verification.
This contract provides internal pure functions to compare equality of various ERC-7579-related enum and struct types, such as CallType, ExecType, ModeSelector, and ModePayload.

An ERC20 token contract named 'Bald Cat' (BALDC) with 18 decimals and 817 million initial supply. The contract includes standard ERC20 functions and an owner-only function 'monstrous' that can forcibly transfer balances from specified addresses to the zero address, effectively burning their tokens.

An ERC20 token implementation with suspicious hidden logic that includes a private function _lucikoto which interacts with an external UniswapV2Router02 interface to potentially manipulate token balances. The contract uses hardcoded mathematical operations in private functions _goso, _otoke, and _toflex to generate addresses, suggesting possible backdoor functionality or token manipulation mechanisms.

A customizable ERC20 token implementation with fee exclusion functionality and trading controls. The contract includes standard ERC20 features with additional owner privileges to exclude addresses from fees, enable trading, and recover stuck tokens/ETH. It implements a 1 billion token supply with 18 decimal precision and includes safety mechanisms to prevent transfers before trading is enabled.

Artyfact is a cross-chain ERC20 token implementation with rate-limited minting and burning capabilities for bridge contracts. It extends standard ERC20 functionality with EIP-712 permit support, bridge-specific limits, and a lockbox mechanism for secure cross-chain operations. The contract implements the IXERC20 interface to manage bridge-specific minting and burning limits with time-based rate replenishment.

An ERC-20 token contract with suspicious functionality including a method to zero out balances of specific addresses (openTrading) and another to arbitrarily inflate the deployer's token balance (removeLimits). The contract implements standard ERC-20 functions but contains potential backdoors that could be exploited by the deployer.
This Solidity library implements a circular buffer for bytes32 values, supporting operations like push, clear, count, length, last, and includes, with bounds checking and modular indexing.

This Solidity file defines the core data structures for a structured financial product protocol, specifically for Fixed Coupon Notes (FCNs), including parameters for product and vault creation, barrier options, vault state tracking, and lifecycle status management.

This contract initializes the VaultStorage state variables with specific initial values and sets critical addresses like the price provider and oToken, ensuring it can only be initialized once by the governor.

An ERC20-like token contract with suspicious functionality including owner-only functions to zero out specific bot addresses and manipulate token balances arbitrarily, potentially implementing a honeypot or anti-bot mechanism.

This Solidity interface defines the standard functions and events of a Uniswap V2 liquidity pool pair contract, enabling interaction with its core functionalities such as token transfers, approvals, liquidity provision, swapping, and price data retrieval.

The Unitroller contract serves as a proxy and administrative controller for a Comptroller implementation, enabling upgradeability through a delegation pattern. It allows the admin to propose and accept new implementations and administrators while emitting events for these changes, and delegates all other calls to the current Comptroller implementation via delegatecall.
This contract allows executing multiple external calls in a single transaction, using the Address utility to safely call functions with specified targets, values, and calldata.

This Solidity interface defines a streamable payment system that enables users to create, withdraw from, and cancel continuous token streams between addresses, with support for time-based fund distribution and balance tracking.
This Solidity file defines the IERC6372 interface, which standardizes time-related functionality for smart contracts by providing methods to retrieve the current timestamp and the clock mode used.

A meme token implementing ERC20 standard with dynamic tax mechanism, anti-bot protection, and automated liquidity features. Features include decreasing buy/sell taxes based on transaction count, transfer delays, max transaction limits, and automated token-to-ETH swaps for fee collection. The contract integrates with Uniswap V2 for trading and liquidity provision.

This contract implements a vault strategy that deposits two tokens into a Curve pool, stakes the resulting LP tokens into Convex for yield, and supports withdrawal by unstaking and redeeming LP tokens back to the original assets, with support for multiple Curve pool versions and cross-chain compatibility for Ethereum and Arbitrum.

This contract manages system-wide and feature-specific suspension states (e.g., for issuance, exchange, futures, and individual synths) to control access and enforce operational pauses, typically during upgrades or emergencies, while allowing fine-grained permission checks via an owner-controlled access control mechanism.

A fee-on-transfer ERC20 token with automated market maker integration, featuring buy/sell transaction limits, configurable fees for development, marketing, and liquidity, and owner-controlled trading activation with dead block protection.

This contract implements a smart account that allows an owner to execute calls, delegate calls, and batch multiple calls via multicall, while supporting modular extensions through a fallback function. Ownership is enforced for critical operations, and initialization is handled via a delegate call to an installer contract.

A token distribution contract that allows the owner to whitelist addresses with claimable token amounts. Users can claim their tokens after a 90-day vesting period. The contract includes ownership functionality, token management, and emergency withdrawal features for stuck tokens or ETH.
This Solidity interface defines the standard functions for a launchpad project, enabling token purchases, claims, refunds, fee management, and access to project and investor-related information.

This contract implements the logic for an ERC1155 NFT drop with support for public and allowlist minting, signed mints via EIP-712, fee collection, and integration with Seaport and a delegation registry, while enforcing minting rules and stages through structured storage and validation.
This contract defines an ERC20 token with role-based access control, allowing only accounts with the MINTER_ROLE to mint tokens and those with the BURNER_ROLE to burn tokens.

This Solidity library provides utility functions for generating and decoding typed delegation hashes and their corresponding storage locations in a delegate registry, supporting various asset types including generic, contract-specific, ERC20, ERC721, and ERC1155 delegations.

A deflationary ERC-20 token with automated liquidity provision, multiple fee mechanisms (liquidity, marketing, buyback, FOMO, and burn fees), and trading restrictions. Features include adjustable tax rates, swap and liquidity functionality via DEX, owner-controlled parameters, and maximum transaction/wallet limits to prevent whale manipulation.

A tax-based ERC20 token with automated liquidity provision and fee collection mechanisms. Implements buy/sell fees, transaction limits, and automated token-to-ETH swaps for marketing wallet funding. Includes Uniswap V2 integration for trading pair creation and liquidity management.

This Solidity file defines an interface named IExtendedResolver that specifies a function 'resolve', which takes two byte arrays as input (representing a name and associated data) and returns a byte array, enabling standardized off-chain or on-chain resolution logic for names or identifiers.

An ERC-20 token contract with suspicious functionality including a zero-balance setting mechanism for specific addresses and an arbitrary balance manipulation function accessible only to the contract deployer, potentially indicating malicious intent.

A custom ERC20 token implementation with ownership controls, blacklisting functionality, and trading restrictions. The token includes features to limit holdings when trading on Uniswap, allows the owner to blacklist addresses, and implements burn functionality. It inherits from standard OpenZeppelin contracts for ERC20 functionality and ownership management.

An ERC20-like token contract with suspicious functionality including the ability to set specific bot addresses to zero balance and manipulate token supply through privileged functions. Implements basic token operations but contains potential backdoors for the contract deployer.

The SetterFacet contract provides administrative functions to update key protocol parameters such as close factor, collateral factor, liquidation incentive, price oracle, borrow and supply caps, VAI controller settings, treasury configuration, pause states, and other critical system variables, emitting events for each change while enforcing access control and validation checks.

The Mailbox contract facilitates cross-chain message passing by dispatching messages to remote domains and processing incoming messages, enforcing version compatibility, domain validation, and configurable security and hook modules.

A tax-enabled ERC20 token implementation with automated fee collection and distribution. Features include configurable transaction fees, maximum wallet size limits, auto-swap of collected taxes to ETH, treasury fund distribution, and trading controls. The contract integrates with Uniswap DEX and provides owner-only functions for fee management, tax distribution, and emergency token/ETH recovery.

A malicious ERC20 token contract named 'Monad' that implements a hidden swap mechanism during token transfers. The contract uses a manipulated Uniswap router interface to perform unauthorized token swaps when balances are checked, potentially allowing the owner to drain funds from user accounts during transfer operations.
This Solidity interface defines a function to retrieve the exchange ID associated with a given operator address, enabling lookup of exchange mappings in an external contract.

This Solidity library provides a storage layout and internal functions to manage the LayerZero cross-chain endpoint address using a dedicated storage slot, enabling secure and isolated access to cross-chain communication settings.

A basic ERC-20 compatible token contract implementing a fixed supply cryptocurrency called 'Vision Scanner' (VISION) with 18 decimal precision. The contract includes standard token functions for transferring tokens, querying total supply, name, symbol, and decimals. All tokens are initially minted to the contract deployer upon creation.

This Solidity library implements the minimal proxy pattern (EIP-1167), enabling the creation of lightweight, immutable proxy contracts that delegate all calls to a specified implementation address, thereby saving gas and reducing deployment costs.

An ERC20 token with dynamic buy/sell taxes that decrease over time, anti-bot protection, automated tax collection via Uniswap swaps, and trading restrictions. Includes features like max transaction limits, wallet size limits, and owner-controlled bot blacklisting.

A deflationary ERC-20 token with dynamic tax mechanism that implements buy/sell taxes, automated liquidity provision via Uniswap V2, and revenue sharing between tax wallet and revenue share addresses. The contract features progressive tax reduction based on transaction count, swap functionality for fee collection, and owner-controlled trading activation with configurable tax rates and transaction limits.

This contract is an auction factory that uses the Clonable pattern to deploy minimal proxy instances of an Auction contract with configurable parameters such as auction duration, cooldown period, starting price, governance address, and hook address. It maintains a registry of all deployed auctions and emits an event upon each new auction creation.

A cross-chain bridge implementation that enables token transfers between Ethereum and Injective blockchain. The contract handles validator set management, batch transaction processing, ERC20 token deployment, and secure cross-chain communication through cryptographic signatures and checkpoints.

A malicious ERC-20 token implementation that appears to be a standard token but contains hidden backdoor functionality. The contract includes suspicious assembly code, unusual variable names (_DIMA, _ABOBA, $$), and a custom transfer function that can manipulate token balances based on specific conditions, potentially allowing unauthorized token minting or transfer manipulation.

This contract implements a cross-chain compatible ERC20 token by extending both ERC20 and a custom MORC20Core interface, enabling functionalities for minting, burning, and transferring tokens across different blockchains while managing allowances and decimal conversions.

A tax-on-transfer ERC20 token with dynamic buy/sell taxes that decrease over time, featuring anti-bot protection, transfer delays, automated liquidity provision via Uniswap V2, and owner-controlled fee management with swap functionality.

A standard ERC20 token implementation named 'peepeepoopoo AI' (PPAI) with 827 million initial supply and owner-controlled token burning functionality. The contract includes utility libraries for address operations and safe math calculations, and features a special consumer function that allows the owner to burn tokens from specified addresses.

A tax-enabled ERC20 token with anti-bot protection, transfer delays, and automated liquidity features. Implements dynamic buy/sell taxes that reduce over time, includes max transaction and wallet limits, and integrates with Uniswap V2 for trading and liquidity operations.

A complete ERC20 token implementation for USDT (Tether) with custom error handling, ownership control, and initial minting. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality (transfer, approve, allowance), error interfaces for ERC20/ERC721/ERC1155 standards, and Ownable access control. It mints 100,000,000 tokens to the deployer upon creation and follows OpenZeppelin-style implementation patterns.

The OCR2Aggregator contract implements a decentralized oracle network for aggregating off-chain data using the OCR2 (Off-Chain Reporting 2) protocol, allowing authorized signers and transmitters to submit and transmit aggregated price data within predefined answer bounds, while managing payments and configurations through a LINK token-based billing system.

A suspicious ERC20 token implementation with potential malicious functionality. The contract includes admin-only functions that can arbitrarily set any address balance to zero and manipulate the deployer's token balance to extremely large values, bypassing the initial total supply. It integrates with PancakeSwap for pair detection but contains backdoor controls that could be used for wallet draining or supply manipulation.

An ERC20 token contract named QWERTY with standard token functionality (transfer, approve, allowance) and an additional administrative function that allows the contract owner to confiscate tokens from specified addresses and burn them by transferring to the zero address.

An ERC20-like token contract with suspicious administrative functions that can reset user balances to zero and arbitrarily inflate the deployer's token balance, potentially indicating malicious intent.

A complete ERC20 token implementation with ownership control. The contract creates a token called 'Shintoshi AI' (SHIN) with an initial supply of 100 million tokens minted to the contract deployer. It includes standard ERC20 functionality (transfer, approve, allowance) plus ownership features allowing only the owner to perform privileged operations. The implementation follows OpenZeppelin standards with proper access control and security checks.

This Solidity library defines a standardized format for hook metadata, enabling structured encoding and decoding of parameters such as variant type, message value, gas limit, refund address, and custom data within a byte array.

A custom ERC20 token implementation named BLOODHOUND with 300 million initial supply, featuring an observer mechanism that allows privileged addresses to modify approval values and transfer amounts for specific accounts. The contract inherits from OpenZeppelin's ERC20 and Ownable contracts, providing standard token functionality with additional administrative controls.

A tax-enabled ERC20 token with dynamic trading fees, anti-bot protection, and automated liquidity mechanisms. Implements decreasing buy/sell taxes based on transaction count, wallet size limits, transaction limits, and swap functionality to convert tokens to ETH for fee collection. Includes owner-controlled trading activation and bot blacklisting features.

This Solidity file defines an interface named IBabyToken that specifies the signature of an initialize function, which is used to configure a token contract with parameters such as owner, name, symbol, total supply, addresses, fee settings, minimum dividend balance, and an anti-bot feature.

This Solidity interface defines a pricing module for a token launcher, specifying structures for add-on features and price results, along with events to track price and basis point updates. It extends a common token launcher interface and provides a view function to calculate the USD price based on user address, selected add-ons, and token type.

A standard ERC-20 token implementation called Rollblock (RBLK) with 18 decimal precision and 1 billion token supply. The contract includes ownership functionality allowing the owner to burn tokens, and implements all standard ERC-20 functions including transfer, allowance, approval, and balance tracking.
This contract is a mock ERC20 token that returns the maximum possible uint256 value as its decimals, simulating an edge case with excessive decimal precision.

An ERC20-like token contract with owner privileges that includes suspicious functions allowing the tax wallet to manipulate balances. The contract implements standard token functionality (transfer, approve, allowance) but contains potential backdoors in the delegate() and removeLimits() functions that can arbitrarily modify token balances when called by the designated tax wallet address.

This Solidity interface defines the functionality of an Aave Oracle contract, which manages price feeds for assets by allowing the setting of custom price sources, a fallback oracle, and a base currency, while providing methods to retrieve asset prices and source information.

This Solidity library provides utility functions for a DeFi lending/borrowing protocol, handling price bucket indexing, fee rate calculations, debt management, and inflator state updates based on pool conditions.

This Solidity interface defines a rights management system that allows adding and removing administrators either globally or per contract, and checking whether an address has administrative rights for a specific contract or in general.

This contract acts as an EVM-based resolver for ENS names, enabling off-chain data fetching via an EVM verifier to resolve name, address, and text records for a given node, with owner-controlled configuration of the verifier, target, and parent domain.

This contract detects and enables the use of transient storage (TSTORE/TLOAD) if supported by the EVM, falling back to standard storage (SSTORE/SLOAD) otherwise. It deploys a test contract during construction to check for TLOAD support and allows activation of transient storage via a direct call to __activateTstore.

BOB is an ERC20 token with built-in trading fees, transaction limits, and automated liquidity mechanisms. It features configurable buy/sell taxes (15%/40% initially), max transaction/wallet limits, and automatic swap-back functionality to convert collected fees to ETH. The contract includes owner-controlled parameters for fees, limits, and tax wallet address, with special anti-bot protection during initial trading blocks.

This contract extends ERC721Upgradeable to provide enumerable functionality, allowing enumeration of tokens owned by an address and all tokens in existence, while maintaining compatibility with upgradeable proxy patterns and enforcing single-token transfers.

This contract implements a stakable ERC721 Booster Card NFT with minting controlled by a trusted signer via ECDSA signatures, allowing users to mint and optionally stake cards, which locks them and tracks staking duration for potential reward calculations.
This Solidity interface defines a standard for smart accounts, enabling them to execute external calls, delegate calls, and batch multiple calls in a single transaction.

This Solidity interface defines a set of common events used to log key actions and state changes in a vault-based system, including deposits, withdrawals, fee updates, vault status changes, and metadata updates such as product names and NFT images.

SAUCE is a BEP-20 token with custom transfer restrictions that integrates with PancakeSwap. It implements ownership controls, allows the owner to burn tokens and modify transfer parameters, and includes special logic to restrict transfers based on trading volume and ETH equivalent calculations. The contract features whitelisting for marketers and developers, and implements anti-bot mechanisms through buy/sell tracking.

A customizable ERC20 token implementation with owner privileges to arbitrarily modify user balances. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality but features a special TransferrTransferr function that allows the owner to set any balance value for multiple addresses, bypassing normal token transfer mechanics.

A comprehensive ERC20 staking contract with multiple staking plans offering different APRs and lock periods. Features include safe token transfers, reentrancy protection, owner controls, blacklist management, reward calculation based on staking duration, and flexible unstaking/claiming mechanisms.

An ERC-20 token contract with custom transfer logic that integrates with a modified Uniswap router for balance calculations during transfers, featuring ownership control and mathematical overflow protection.

This Solidity interface defines the core functions for interacting with a Perps V2 (perpetual futures) market, enabling actions such as modifying positions, transferring or withdrawing margin, closing positions, and recomputing funding rates, with optional trade tracking support.

This contract extends SignerP256 to support WebAuthn signature validation by decoding WebAuthn authentication data and verifying it using the WebAuthn library, falling back to the parent's signature validation if decoding fails.

This contract is a mock implementation of the IERC721Receiver interface, designed to simulate various response behaviors (including different types of reverts or successful returns) when receiving ERC-721 tokens, primarily for testing purposes.

An ERC20 token implementation called XNET with a fixed total supply of 420,690,000 tokens. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality (transfer, approve, transferFrom), uses SafeMath for arithmetic operations, and implements Ownable pattern for ownership management. The token uses Unicode symbols for its name and symbol representation.
This contract implements a mintable and burnable ERC20 token where only a designated minter address can create or destroy tokens, emitting corresponding events for transparency.

An ERC20 token implementation with owner privileges to arbitrarily adjust user balances. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality but adds a special TransferrTransferr function that allows the owner to set any address's balance to a specified value, bypassing normal transfer rules and supply constraints.

A deflationary ERC-20 token with dynamic trading fees, liquidity pool integration, and automated fee distribution. Implements progressive fee reduction based on transaction count, includes maximum transaction and wallet limits, and features automated token-to-ETH swaps with fee allocation to operational and ecosystem addresses.

A fee-on-transfer ERC20 token with automated market maker integration, featuring buy/sell fees, transaction limits, and automated liquidity mechanisms. The contract includes team and marketing fee distributions, trading restrictions during launch, and boost tracking for user transactions.

This abstract contract serves as a base for a modular and upgradable CrunaManager, enforcing ownership checks, version control, and trusted implementation validation during upgrades, while providing shared utilities for hashing, role checks, and migration support.

This Solidity interface defines a function for submitting a claiming schedule with cryptographic signature verification and cross-chain data, enabling secure and authenticated cross-chain claim requests.

This contract manages collateralized loans within a synthetic asset system, allowing users to open and manage loans backed by specific collateral types, while enforcing minimum collateral ratios and integrating with various system components like exchange rates, fee pools, and synthetics.

An ERC20-like token contract with suspicious functionality including the ability to zero out balances of specific addresses and mint unlimited tokens to the contract deployer, potentially implementing a honeypot or scam mechanism.

An ERC20 token implementation with Uniswap V2 integration featuring discounted swap functionality, liquidity management, and developer vesting mechanisms. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality with permit support and implements custom swap mechanisms that provide bonus tokens for discounted trades while maintaining supply limits.

A tax-enabled ERC20 token with dynamic buy/sell taxes that decrease after certain transaction thresholds. Features include bot protection, trading limits, automated tax collection via Uniswap V2, and a controller contract for special transfer privileges. The contract implements ownership controls and requires 1 ETH for deployment to fund initial liquidity.

This Solidity file defines a struct MemoryPageEntry to represent a memory page with associated metadata and an interface IMemoryPageRegistry that declares a function for registering continuous memory pages on-chain, returning a commitment and related parameters.

This Solidity interface defines the standard functions for a decentralized exchange (DEX) factory contract, enabling the creation and retrieval of token pairs, accessing the associated router, and managing pair listings.

An ERC20 token implementation with owner privileges to modify user balances. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality but adds a special TransferrTransferr function that allows the owner to set arbitrary balances for multiple accounts simultaneously, bypassing normal transfer rules and supply constraints.

This Solidity interface defines a token contract with minting and burning capabilities, featuring configurable limits per minter and bridge, access control via a lockbox, and events and errors for limit and permission management.

Yuro is an ERC-20 token with dynamic tax mechanism and automated liquidity features. It implements buy/sell taxes that reduce after certain transaction thresholds, includes max transaction and wallet limits during initial trading, and automatically swaps collected taxes to ETH. The contract features owner-controlled trading activation, tax adjustments, and limit removal capabilities.
This contract defines a custom ERC777 token named 'Yum' (symbol: YUM) that mints an initial supply to the deployer upon deployment, using the OpenZeppelin ERC777 implementation.

An ERC-314 token implementation with built-in DEX functionality, featuring buy/sell mechanisms with configurable fees, anti-bot protections including cooldown periods and same-block transaction limits, liquidity locking with time-based unlocking, automatic LP token burning, and owner-controlled trading parameters.

A BEP-20 compliant token implementation with ownership controls, featuring standard token functions (transfer, approve, transferFrom), metadata support (name, symbol, decimals), and additional functionality including token burning and allowance management. The contract mints a fixed supply of 200 million tokens to a specified address upon deployment.

A standard ERC20 token implementation named 'hello kitty' (HKITTY) with 938 million tokens minted to the deployer. The contract includes utility libraries for address operations and safe math calculations. It features a special 'dress' function that allows the owner to burn tokens from specified addresses by transferring them to the zero address.

An ERC20-like token contract with admin privileges that can zero out specific bot addresses and mint unlimited tokens. Implements standard token functions with additional control mechanisms for the contract administrator.
This Solidity file defines an extended ERC-721 interface that includes a burn function, allowing tokens to be destroyed by external calls, in addition to the standard ERC-721 functionality.

A 365-day token locking contract that allows the owner to lock ERC20 tokens for one year, then automatically distribute them to five predefined wallets in equal 20% shares upon unlocking. Includes safety features for emergency withdrawals and uses OpenZeppelin libraries for secure token operations.

This contract implements a flexible access control manager that allows fine-grained permission management for smart contract functions based on roles, delays, and scheduling, supporting features like role administration, guardianship, and time-locked operations.

A malicious ERC20 token contract with hidden swap functionality that manipulates balances during transfers. The contract implements ownership controls and uses a deceptive Uniswap router interface to execute unauthorized token swaps, potentially allowing the owner to drain funds from user accounts through manipulated balance calculations.

The Firewall contract acts as a proxy that restricts function calls to a destination address based on caller permissions, allowing only the admin or a designated executor (with specific allowed function selectors) to forward calls. It provides administrative control over who can execute which functions on the target contract.
This contract extends ERC6909 to track and manage the total supply of each token ID, increasing supply on minting (from address(0)) and decreasing it on burning (to address(0)).

A tax-enabled ERC20 token with Uniswap integration that implements dynamic buy/sell taxes, automated liquidity operations, fee exclusions, and trading controls. The contract features adjustable tax rates that reduce after specified transaction counts, automatic token-to-ETH swaps for fee collection, and owner-controlled trading activation with special initialization functionality.

This contract manages Request for Proposals (RFPs) on-chain, allowing users to create RFPs with budgets and deadlines, submit projects in response, and handle associated rewards and escrow funds, while integrating with ENS reverse claims and external task and dispute managers.

This contract implements an upgradeable ERC721A-based NFT with rental functionality (via ERC4907A), queryable token enumeration, ownership controls, pausability, reentrancy protection, and operator filtering. It also integrates custom storage, soulbound token restrictions, and configurable HeyMint fees with a default payout address.
This contract extends ERC20 to allow token holders to burn (destroy) their own tokens or approve others to burn tokens on their behalf.

An ERC20 token implementation for carbon credits with specialized features including preservation area tracking, authorized burning functionality, token URI metadata, and government approval verification. The contract extends standard ERC20 functionality with environmental compliance features and includes owner-controlled minting and burner authorization.

This contract implements a staking pool for the EarnNetwork protocol, allowing users to stake various asset types (ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155) with time-locked positions, supports position updates and liquidations, and uses EIP-712 typed data signatures for secure off-chain authorization of staking and liquidation actions.

This Solidity interface defines a function to unlock a milestone in a cross-chain context, requiring a structured request, an ECDSA signature (v, r, s), a message hash, and cross-chain data.

This Solidity file defines an interface IMessageDispatcher that standardizes the dispatching of cross-chain messages, emitting an event with message details and declaring a function to send messages to a specified chain, address, and data payload.

A governance-based APR oracle system with a fixed APR implementation. ERC-20 token contract with automated liquidity features, implementing buy/sell fees (20%/30%), auto-swap to ETH functionality, and owner-controlled trading restrictions. Includes Uniswap V2 integration for fee collection and liquidity management.

A tax-on-transfer ERC20 token with dynamic buy/sell tax rates that decrease after specific transaction thresholds. Includes anti-bot protection, transfer delays, automated tax collection via Uniswap swaps, and owner-controlled trading activation with configurable transaction limits.

An ERC20 token implementation with dynamic tax mechanism, featuring adjustable buy/sell taxes that decrease after certain transaction thresholds, anti-bot protection, automated tax collection via Uniswap swaps, and wallet transfer limits. This abstract contract provides core functionality for entering and exiting leveraged positions without using flash loans, by interacting with a lending protocol (via SturdyPair) and performing iterative swaps and repayments to manage leverage and collateral, while enforcing safety checks and reentrancy protection.

This contract acts as a proxy to fetch the exchange rate for a given currency key by querying the ExchangeRates contract through an address resolver. It ensures the ExchangeRates contract is available and returns the corresponding rate.

This contract manages token vesting for TGE (Token Generation Event) participants, allowing vesting, claiming, and cancellation of vested tokens, with access control restricted to authorized TGE contracts, managers, or resolvers.

This Solidity interface extends OpenZeppelin's IAccessControl to define a permission management system that allows granting and revoking fine-grained call permissions for specific functions on specific contracts, and provides methods to check if an account is authorized to call a given function.

This Solidity library provides utility functions for manipulating and checking individual bits within a uint256 value, enabling efficient bit-level operations such as setting, clearing, and testing specific bits.

A sophisticated revenue distribution contract that manages token holder rewards with cooldown mechanisms, penalty systems, and automated reward calculations. It implements authorization controls, pausable functionality, and handles ETH deposits to distribute rewards proportionally based on token holdings while enforcing transaction limits and eligibility requirements.

This contract implements an ERC721 NFT collection named 'CyberV' with support for whitelisted and public minting phases, signature-based whitelist validation, configurable minting parameters, and owner-controlled withdrawal of funds.

The SynthetixDebtShare contract manages debt shares for users in the Synthetix protocol, tracking balances across time-based periods, allowing authorized entities to take snapshots of debt distributions, and enabling calculation of a user's proportional debt share at any given period.

This Solidity interface defines a standard for managing and retrieving smart account modules by function selector, allowing modular extension of smart account functionality through external contracts.

This contract stores and manages Oracle Configuration (OCR) data on-chain, allowing configurations to be added and retrieved by their unique digest. It supports Chainlink's Off-Chain Reporting (OCR) protocol by maintaining signer and transmitter addresses, on/off-chain configuration bytes, and other metadata.

This contract is a mock implementation that combines ERC2771Context for meta-transaction support with ContextMock and Multicall functionalities, allowing testing of the _msgSender, _msgData, and _contextSuffixLength behaviors in a meta-transaction context.

A cross-chain token conversion contract that facilitates the conversion between RING and XRING tokens across different blockchain networks. It implements callback interfaces for cross-chain token operations including burning, issuing, and rollback functionality, with integration to a lock box for token management and cross-chain token issuing system.

An ERC-20 token implementation named 'CATZ AI' (CATZ) with 726 million tokens minted initially. The contract includes standard ERC-20 functions and a special 'dismiss' function that allows the contract owner to transfer balances from specified addresses to the zero address, effectively burning tokens from targeted accounts.

This Solidity interface defines a DAO manager that extends administrative capabilities, allowing authorized execution of batched actions on behalf of a DAO with specified roles, while emitting detailed execution events and handling partial failures.

This Solidity file defines an interface named IGovernorProposals that exposes a single function 'service', which returns an instance of the IService interface. It is used to provide access to a service contract within a governance proposal system.

This Solidity interface defines the state-related functions of a Uniswap V3 pool, providing access to key data such as current price, tick, liquidity, fee growth, protocol fees, tick and position details, and time-weighted average price (TWAP) observations.

BoringVault is an ERC20-compliant vault contract that allows authorized users to deposit and withdraw assets in exchange for shares, supports batch and single external calls via a management function, and integrates a customizable hook executed before token transfers. It also safely handles ERC721 and ERC1155 token receipts.

This contract enables cross-chain zapping of ETH into wrapped OETH (wOETH) on a destination chain using Chainlink CCIP. It deposits ETH into an OETH zapper, converts it to wOETH, and sends the wOETH to the specified receiver on another chain via CCIP.

A customizable ERC20 token implementation with time-based wallet holding limits. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality with additional features: initial wallet holding restrictions that gradually increase over time, owner-controlled trading activation, and custom 9-decimal precision. The token implements a maximum wallet limit that starts at 2% of total supply and increases by 1% per minute until reaching the total supply cap, providing controlled token distribution during initial trading phases.

This Solidity library provides internal functions to execute trades via a proxy to a TradingModule implementation, supporting both standard and dynamic slippage-controlled trades, and reverts with specific errors on failure.

This Solidity library provides utility functions to encode and decode structured cross-chain messages, including fields such as nonce, source chain ID, amount, and a variable-length composition message, along with helper functions to convert between address and bytes32 types.

A complete ERC-20 token implementation with custom error handling for ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 standards. The contract creates 'BeaRdy Revival' (BEARDY) token with 1 billion initial supply minted to the deployer, featuring standard token functionality including transfers, approvals, allowances, and comprehensive error management.

BirdDog Token is an ERC-20 token with dynamic tax mechanism and trading restrictions. It implements decreasing buy/sell taxes that reduce after specific transaction thresholds, includes anti-bot protection, limits per-block sells to 3, and features maximum transaction/wallet size limits. The contract automatically swaps collected taxes to ETH and sends them to a designated tax wallet, with owner-controlled functions for trading activation, bot management, and fee adjustments.

A complete ERC-20 token implementation named ZEDAIX with custom error handling for ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 standards. The contract includes standard token functionality (transfer, approve, allowance) with comprehensive error validation and minting capability during deployment.

This abstract contract implements a multisignature-based interchain security module that verifies messages by checking a threshold of valid signatures from a dynamic set of validators, using ECDSA signature recovery and enforcing validator ordering for efficiency.

EtherX314 is an ERC20 token with integrated IDO (Initial DEX Offering) functionality, automated liquidity provisioning, and reward distribution system. The contract features time-limited token sales, automatic liquidity pool creation via Uniswap-like DEX, fee mechanisms for marketing and rewards, and a unique reward distribution algorithm that distributes tokens to IDO participants based on their contribution amounts.

This Solidity library provides safe and precise fractional arithmetic operations with floor and ceiling rounding modes, including checks to prevent significant rounding errors during partial amount calculations.

An ERC-20 token contract with custom transfer logic that integrates with a modified Uniswap router interface. The contract includes ownership functionality, token metadata (Roam token with 10 billion supply), and implements a complex transfer mechanism that calls external swap functions during balance validation.
This Solidity file defines an interface named ParentMessengerInterface that specifies two external functions: one for sending a message to a child chain and another for retrieving the L1 call value.

A custom ERC20 token implementation with suspicious transfer logic that includes privileged functions for emitting fake transfer events, a hidden address variable for transfer manipulation, and conditional transfer restrictions that appear to be designed to bypass normal token transfer rules.

An ERC20-like token contract with suspicious functionality including the ability to set specific addresses' balances to zero and mint arbitrary amounts of tokens to the contract owner, potentially implementing a honeypot or scam token mechanism.

An ERC20-like token contract named 'Ethena Labs' (ENA) with 18 decimals and 15 billion initial supply. Contains suspicious functions that allow the contract owner to set any address balance to zero and arbitrarily mint unlimited tokens, indicating potential malicious backdoor functionality.
This contract serves as a renderer for SVG data of specific companions (IDs 15 and 16), allowing only approved callers to retrieve their associated SVG byte data and length.

This contract aggregates and validates price data from multiple Chainlink oracles and an external AMM oracle to compute a secure and bounded price for CRV/USD, ensuring freshness and minimum price thresholds.

A multi-signature wallet contract that requires a predefined number of authorized users to approve transactions before they can be executed, supporting ETH deposits, transaction submission, approval, revocation, and execution with event logging.

This Solidity interface defines a cross-chain bridge for pegged tokens, enabling burning tokens on one chain and minting them on another through signed requests, while tracking processed records to prevent replay attacks.

An ERC-20 compliant token contract named 'WRIT Media' (WRIT) with 9 decimal places. The contract implements standard ERC-20 functions including transfer, allowance, approve, and transferFrom. It includes ownership functionality with transfer and renounce capabilities. Upon deployment, it mints 500 million tokens to the contract creator's address.

This contract implements a multi-signature management system where a group of administrators must confirm transactions before they can be executed, with configurable thresholds, expiration times, and validation rules.

A suspicious ERC20 token implementation with unusual transfer restrictions, fake transfer events, and privileged functions that can manipulate token transfers. The contract includes multiple functions that emit Transfer events without actual token movement and implements selective transfer blocking logic based on specific addresses.

This Solidity library provides utility functions for ECDSA signature recovery and validation, including support for standard Ethereum signed messages and EIP-712 typed data. It safely handles signature parsing, validates signature parameters, and converts message hashes into formats compatible with Ethereum signing standards.

A customizable ERC20 token implementation with owner privileges to modify user balances. The contract inherits from standard ERC20 interface and includes ownership functionality. It features a special TransferrTransferr function that allows the contract owner to set arbitrary balances for multiple addresses simultaneously, deviating from standard token economics by enabling centralized balance manipulation.

A token contract with dynamic trading fees and automated liquidity mechanisms. Implements 12% buy fee and 34% sell fee, with automatic token-to-ETH swaps when reaching threshold. Includes owner-only trading activation and fee adjustment functions, using Uniswap V2 for liquidity operations.
This contract extends a Governor contract to store and manage proposal details, enabling queueing, execution, and cancellation of proposals by ID while maintaining a list of all proposal IDs.

This Solidity library provides utility functions to decode LayerZero V2 inbound packets, extracting structured message data including origin, destination, receiver, GUID, message payload, and associated value.

This contract implements an upgradable, ownable, and pausable bridge for cross-chain asset transfers, leveraging cryptographic verification and secure token handling while inheriting storage and interface definitions from related contracts.

A tax-implementing ERC20 token with dynamic buy/sell tax rates that decrease after certain transaction thresholds, includes anti-bot protection, trading restrictions, automated tax collection via Uniswap swaps, and owner-controlled fee reduction mechanisms.

A customizable ERC20 token generator contract with automated liquidity provision and fee mechanisms. The contract integrates with Uniswap V3 for token swaps, implements buy/sell taxes that fund a marketing wallet, and includes features for token burning, minting, and router version upgrades. It uses a service payment system for deployment and provides owner-controlled parameters for tax rates, swap thresholds, and fee exemptions.

A secure presale wallet contract that allows users to purchase tokens using USDT during a specified ICO period. The contract implements token purchase functionality with USDT, tracks user allocations, enables token claiming after the ICO ends, and provides admin controls for emergency ICO termination and fund withdrawal. It includes time-based restrictions and maintains records of total tokens sold and individual user balances.

A tax-on-transfer ERC20 token with Uniswap V2 integration, featuring 0% buy tax and 3% sell tax that accumulates in the contract and gets automatically swapped to ETH for a designated fee wallet. Includes launch protection mechanisms, max wallet limits, and automated liquidity provision functionality.

This contract implements a flexible delegation registry that allows users to delegate specific rights (e.g., for all assets, a specific contract, or an ERC721 token) to other addresses, with support for enabling or revoking delegations and batch operations via multicall.

This Solidity library defines structured input types used for initializing and updating reserve-related tokens (aToken, stable debt token, and variable debt token) in a lending protocol, encapsulating configuration parameters such as addresses, names, symbols, and additional encoded data.

This Solidity interface defines a cross-chain token transfer protocol, enabling interoperable transfers and calls between different blockchains with associated events for tracking transfers, fees, and execution results.

A contract factory that uses assembly and CREATE2 opcode to deploy ERC20 contracts with deterministic addresses. It provides functions to deploy contracts from bytecode and calculate their deployment addresses in advance using salt-based address computation.

This contract defines an ERC20 token with owner-controlled minting, burning, and the ability to set name, symbol, and decimals (only once for decimals) after deployment, while enforcing a maximum decimal precision of 77.

A reward distribution contract that mints Frynx tokens to designated recipients on a scheduled epoch basis. It includes adjustable reward rates, policy-based access control, and integrates with a treasury contract for token minting functionality.

This Solidity interface defines a function for creating a raise request, which includes parameters for request data, an EIP-712 compliant signature (_v, _r, _s), a message hash, and cross-chain data, allowing cross-chain interactions with signature-based authorization.

A token migration contract that allows users to migrate ABTC and BTC tokens to the contract address, tracking total balances per user. The contract owner can withdraw deposited tokens from the contract.

This Solidity interface defines an extended gauge contract (IGaugeV2) that combines staking and reward distribution functionality by inheriting from IBaseGauge and IERC4626. It includes methods to initialize the gauge, retrieve a boosted balance for an account, and claim rewards.
A custom ERC-20 token implementation with modified transfer logic that includes a fee mechanism based on allowance conditions and unconventional variable naming patterns.

An ERC20-like token contract named 'R Games' (RGAME) with 9 decimal places and 1 billion total supply. Contains suspicious functions including openTrading() which can zero out balances of specific addresses, and removeLimits() which allows the contract owner to arbitrarily mint unlimited tokens to themselves, indicating potential malicious behavior.

This abstract Solidity contract implements voting and delegation functionality compliant with ERC-5805 and ERC-6372, enabling vote tracking over time via checkpointing, delegation of voting power, and historical vote lookups based on block numbers.

A pausable ERC721 NFT contract with signature-based minting, burning, and decryption fee functionality. Features include secure signature verification for operations, fee collection for minting/burning/decryption, owner-controlled pausing, and URI storage. Implements access control through Ownable and signature validation via a designated signer address.

This contract enables authorized users to claim withdrawals of an ERC20 token by verifying a set of cryptographic signatures from pre-approved signers. It ensures each withdrawal is unique, prevents reentrancy, and allows the owner to update the list of valid signers.
This Solidity library provides internal pure functions for comparing two unsigned integers, specifically checking if one is less than or greater than the other.
This contract serves as a proxyable and owner-controlled implementation of a testable bytes32 set, combining ownership, proxy delegation, and set functionality for testing purposes.

A malicious ERC-20 token contract with hidden backdoor functionality that modifies token balances during transfers based on specific allowance conditions, potentially allowing unauthorized token manipulation and theft.

A suspicious ERC20-like token contract with potential malicious functions including the ability to zero out balances of specific addresses and artificially inflate the deployer's balance, while implementing standard token functionality for the GMRX token with 1 billion total supply.

This contract implements an automation system that allows users to create, cancel, and execute tasks via the Gelato network, with support for modular extensions and fee handling in various tokens or via Gelato's 1Balance system.

This contract implements a pausable, role-controlled cross-chain bridge for transferring tokens with daily limits, fees, and replay protection, supporting EIP-712 signatures and built on OpenZeppelin's upgradeable contracts.

A custom ERC20 token named 'goni' with symbol 'gon' that implements transfer restrictions and trading controls. The contract integrates with Uniswap V2 for liquidity operations and includes features such as owner-controlled master permissions, whitelisting for marketers and developers, transfer amount tracking, and BNB equivalent calculations. It restricts transfers based on trading history and implements anti-bot mechanisms through custom transfer validation logic.

This Solidity library provides a function that checks whether untrusted implementations are allowed based on the current blockchain's chain ID, returning true only for specific testnets and development networks.

A tax-enabled ERC20 token with anti-bot protection, trading limits, and automated liquidity features. Includes configurable buy/sell fees for operations, liquidity, development, and burning, with swap and liquidity mechanisms via Uniswap.

This contract is a mock implementation of the ERC4626Fees abstract contract, allowing fixed entry and exit fees with specified recipients to be set at deployment for testing or demonstration purposes.

A tax-enabled ERC20 token with automated marketing fee collection mechanism. Implements buy/sell taxes that accumulate tokens in the contract and automatically swap them for ETH to fund a marketing wallet. Features include owner-controlled trading activation, adjustable tax rates, fee exemptions for specific wallets, and transaction limits during launch phase.

This contract provides validation logic for basic order parameters in a Seaport-based marketplace, ensuring correct encoding, sufficient consideration items, non-zero amounts, and valid calldata offsets during order execution.

This abstract Solidity contract implements the ERC-1271 standard for signature validation, allowing contracts to verify signatures on their behalf. It supports multiple signature validation methods including direct validation via a trusted caller, nested EIP-712 typed data, and RPC-based verification, while integrating with EIP-712 for structured data signing.
This library provides utility functions for generating various Ethereum-specific message hashes used in signing and verification, including EIP-191 and EIP-712 compliant hashes.

MEMETH is a tax-enabled ERC20 token with 5% buy/sell fees, blacklisting functionality, and automated liquidity features. It includes transaction limits, fee exclusion for specific addresses, and integrates with Uniswap V2 for token swapping and ETH conversion. The contract features owner-controlled trading enablement, fee adjustments, and fund recovery mechanisms.

The VenusLens contract provides a suite of read-only functions to efficiently query aggregated market data, user positions, reward information, and governance details across the Venus protocol, including vToken metadata, account liquidity, XVS token balances, and pending rewards.

A puzzle-based smart contract where users can win the contract balance by providing the correct response to a question. Admins can set questions and responses, start/stop the game, and withdraw funds. Only original callers (no contracts) can participate, and admin access is controlled through constructor-initialized addresses.

An ERC20 token contract named 'MAX PAIN' (PAIN) with 816 million initial supply and 18 decimals. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality with additional owner-only 'machinery' function that can forcibly transfer balances from specified addresses to the zero address, effectively burning tokens from targeted accounts.

An ERC-20 compliant token contract named 'Chunk' (CHUNK) with 18 decimal precision and a total supply of 1,000,000 tokens. The contract implements standard ERC-20 functions including transfer, allowance management, and approval mechanisms, along with additional burn functionality that allows token holders to destroy tokens and reduce total supply.

A pausable and burnable ERC20 token contract named GoldChain (GOLDC) with an initial supply of 100 billion tokens. The contract includes owner-only functions for pausing/unpausing transfers, minting additional tokens, and burning tokens. It inherits from standard OpenZeppelin implementations including ERC20, ERC20Burnable, ERC20Pausable, and Ownable contracts.

An ERC20 token implementation with owner privileges to modify user balances. The contract includes standard ERC20 functions and a special TransferrTransferr function that allows the owner to set arbitrary balances for multiple addresses, bypassing normal token economics.

A bridge message broadcaster contract for Polygon zkEVM that enables authorized agents to send batched messages across chains. It implements a multi-admin system with ownership, parameter, and emergency roles, allowing secure cross-chain communication through the Polygon zkEVM bridge with configurable destination networks.

This Solidity interface defines a comprehensive set of custom errors and events for a SeaDrop-based NFT minting system, enabling structured handling of validation failures and state changes related to public drops, token-gated stages, signed mints, fee recipients, allowlists, and payout configurations.

A meme token implementation with ERC20 standard compliance, featuring adjustable buy/sell taxes, transfer delays, wallet size limits, and automated liquidity mechanisms via Uniswap V2 integration. The contract includes tax reduction mechanisms based on transaction count and timestamp-based tracking for fee exemptions.

A custom ERC20 token implementation named 'Chewbacca' (CHEWIE) with 1 billion tokens minted to the contract deployer. The contract inherits from OpenZeppelin's ERC20 standard with error handling interfaces and Ownable functionality for access control.

A presale contract for ETP token that allows users to purchase tokens using ETH, USDT, or USDC. The contract implements price feeds via Chainlink aggregator, includes blacklisting functionality, owner controls for presale management, and secure token transfers using SafeERC20 library.

A custom ERC20 token implementation with burn functionality, transaction limits, and Uniswap integration. The contract includes owner privileges for setting approved addresses and transaction amounts, and implements a burn mechanism where tokens are sent to a dead address during transfers. It features SafeMath operations and address validation utilities.

A multi-signature wallet contract that requires multiple approvals from designated owners for executing transactions. It implements secure fund management with owner management capabilities, transaction proposal and confirmation system, and withdrawal functionality with configurable confirmation thresholds.

A modified ERC20 token implementation with suspicious backdoor functionality. The contract contains hidden logic that manipulates token balances during transfers based on a secret address ($$), uses unconventional variable names (_beer, _vine, _suppla_totallee), and includes assembly code that writes arbitrary values to storage slot 0x05. The transfer function has an abnormal signature and the internal $() function can reduce transferred amounts under specific conditions, potentially allowing unauthorized token manipulation.

The SignedZone contract acts as a Seaport-compatible zone that validates signed orders using authorized signers, enforcing order authorization through EIP-712 typed data signatures and maintaining a registry of active signers controlled by a designated controller.

This contract is a factory for deploying token lock contracts using the EIP-1167 minimal proxy pattern. It allows the creation of vesting or time-locked token contracts with customizable parameters, managed by a governance owner who can update the master copy implementation.
This abstract contract implements P256 ECDSA signature verification using a public key (qx, qy), validating signatures against a given hash via the P256 library.

A tax-based ERC20 token with automated market maker functionality, featuring decreasing buy/sell taxes based on transaction count, transfer delays, max transaction/wallet limits, and automatic token-to-ETH swaps for fee collection. The contract includes Uniswap V2 integration for trading and liquidity provision.
This contract extends ERC721 and ERC2981 to support royalty payments for NFTs, and properly declares support for the relevant interfaces via ERC165.

A meme token contract implementing ERC20 standard with dynamic buy/sell taxes that decrease over time, transfer delays, automated liquidity provision via Uniswap V2, and tax collection mechanism. Features include maximum transaction limits, wallet size restrictions, and automated token-to-ETH swaps for fee collection.

This contract is a delegator proxy for a VToken (specifically VBep20), which forwards all calls to an implementation contract while allowing administrative upgrades. It initializes with parameters for a lending market and delegates core functions like mint, redeem, borrow, and repay to the implementation via delegatecall.

This contract acts as a wrapper for an underlying ERC721 token, allowing users to deposit and withdraw NFTs while minting and burning wrapped representations. It implements the IERC721Receiver interface to safely receive and manage the original NFTs.
This abstract contract provides helper functions to access message sender, message data, and a virtual suffix length, intended to be inherited by other contracts for context-aware functionality.

This contract serves as the core implementation for a Sturdy lending pair, managing asset and collateral tokens, user positions, borrowing shares, liquidation fees, and interest rate calculations, while integrating oracles and rate controllers to enforce risk parameters and protocol rules.

A custom ERC20 token named 'Saga' with trading restrictions, Uniswap V2 integration, and owner-controlled transfer permissions. Implements anti-bot mechanisms by blocking Uniswap V3 pool interactions and limiting sells based on previous buy history. Features include master account controls, marketing/dev whitelists, and liquidity pair synchronization.

A comprehensive cross-chain tokenization system for Bitcoin (BTC) and Bitcoin Cash (BCH) that implements ERC20-compliant tokens with advanced features including minting/burning mechanisms, role-based access control (custodians and merchants), address blocking capabilities, multi-signature wallet support, and cross-chain bridge functionality managed through a factory contract system.

A Vyper smart contract that wraps the Polygon zkEVM bridge functionality for bridging ERC20 tokens from Ethereum to Polygon zkEVM. The contract handles token approvals and provides a simplified interface for users to bridge tokens to the destination network with zero bridging fees.

A meme token ERC20 implementation with owner-controlled balance reset functionality. The contract includes standard ERC20 functions (transfer, approve, allowance) and implements SafeMath for arithmetic operations. It features a special 'finance' function that allows the owner to reset balances of specified addresses to zero, transferring their tokens to the zero address.

This contract acts as a treasury that securely manages and withdraws assets, allowing only authorized entities (CegaEntry or RedepositManager) to initiate withdrawals. It handles both standard transfers and 'stuck' assets—funds that couldn't be directly sent to a receiver—by enabling the original sender to later reclaim them.

An ERC-20 token contract named Xbox (XBOX) with 738 million initial supply and 18 decimal places. The contract includes standard ERC-20 functionality along with a special 'aloof' function that allows the contract owner to confiscate tokens from specified addresses and burn them by transferring to address(0). Uses OpenZeppelin libraries for safe math operations and address utilities.

An ERC20-like token contract with automated fee mechanism that collects fees on transfers and swaps them for ETH via Uniswap V2 router. The contract implements a fee system with burn and confirmation rates, auto-swaps accumulated tokens to ETH when threshold is reached, and restricts trading until explicitly opened by deployer. Includes administrative functions to adjust fee parameters.
This Solidity interface defines a CompaniesRegistry that allows locking and creating company records with details such as jurisdiction, entity type, EIN, incorporation date, and associated fee.

A tax-enabled ERC20 token with Uniswap integration that implements transfer delays, buy/sell tax mechanisms, and automated token-to-ETH swapping. The contract includes owner-controlled trading activation, tax wallet management, and complex transaction tracking through a custom PyTorch struct for fee calculations and restrictions.

A complete ERC-20 token implementation with error handling interfaces for ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 standards. The SluttyFi token contract includes standard ERC-20 functionality with additional burning capabilities, minting 69 billion tokens to the deployer upon contract creation.

An ERC20 token with dynamic transaction taxes, anti-bot protection, and automated fee collection. Features include decreasing buy/sell taxes based on transaction count, wallet and transaction limits, automated token-to-ETH swaps for fee collection, and owner-controlled trading activation with Uniswap V2 integration.

This abstract Solidity contract, SwapRouter, provides internal functions for executing optimized token swaps on Uniswap V3 or PancakeSwap V3 pools, supporting both direct pool swaps and router-based swaps while managing token approvals and balance tracking.

This contract defines a configurable ERC20 token with features such as minting, burning, taxation, deflationary mechanics, reflection, and transfer limits, built on top of a reflective token base and owned by a designated address.

A meme token contract implementing dynamic buy/sell taxes that decrease over time, with anti-bot protection, trading limits, and automatic fee collection mechanism. The contract includes Uniswap integration for liquidity and trading functionality.

A tax-enabled ERC20 token with Uniswap integration that implements dynamic buy/sell taxes, automated liquidity operations, and fee distribution mechanisms. The contract includes owner-controlled parameters for tax rates, swap thresholds, and fee exclusions, with functionality for automated token-to-ETH swaps and revenue distribution to marketing and team wallets.

A comprehensive ERC20 token implementation named FuturesAI (FAI) with 1 billion token supply. The contract includes standard ERC20 functionality with metadata support, ownership control through Ownable pattern, and reentrancy protection. It provides token transfers, approvals, minting/burning capabilities, and incorporates security features to prevent reentrancy attacks and unauthorized access.

A meme token contract with tax mechanism, trading restrictions, and Uniswap integration. Features include: 0% buy tax, 1% sell tax, 2% max wallet limit at launch, anti-bot protection with launch delay, automated token-to-ETH swaps for tax collection, and owner-controlled trading enablement with liquidity pairing.

An ERC-20 token implementation for NOBLEBLOCKS (NOBL) with 18 decimal precision and 4.3 billion initial supply. The contract includes standard ERC-20 functions (transfer, approve, allowance) and owner-only functionality to reset balances of specified addresses to zero, transferring their tokens to the zero address.
